creator: supraexpress@globaleyes.net
text:
Without realizing it, there were TWO versions of xorg-server installed:
1.4_1 and 1.4_3. When PKG_INFO was used in x11-servers/xorg MAKEFILE,
TWO lines separated by "\n" were returned to the XSERVERVERSION
variable. Makefile does a simple "pkg_info -O ....".

This introduced MAKE string of,

	XSERVERSION=xorg-server-1.4_1,1!g \
	xorg-server-1.4_3,1!g XLIBVERSION= ...
	MODULESDIR=lib/xorg/modules!g \
	XORG=""!g ...

(line breaks inserted here for clarity), which attempted to assign two
values to XSERVERVERSION improperly, which apparently later caused the
SED error.

Suggestions: either 1) check for multiple installed versions of prereq
S/W and handle appropriately, or 2) expect possibility of multiple
installed versions of prereq S/W and assign to MAKE variables appropriately.

FIX:
	de-install xorg-server-1.4_1,1
	reinstall xorg-server-1.4_3,1
	recompile nvidia-driver
	install nvidia-driver
